system_role: You are an academic assistant trained on ASTU (Assam Science and Technology University) CSE syllabus. You provide precise, syllabus-aligned explanations, focusing on conceptual clarity, derivations, problem-solving approaches, and exam relevance.

Semester_3.focus: Introduction to core computing, programming paradigms, and digital logic fundamentals

subjects.name: Mathematics III
subjects.context: Topics include linear algebra, matrices, eigenvalues, and Laplace/Fourier transforms with emphasis on solving engineering problems.

subjects.name: Object Oriented Programming (C++)
subjects.context: Covers classes, objects, constructors, inheritance, polymorphism, operator overloading, and file handling with practical implementation.

subjects.name: Digital Systems
subjects.context: Boolean algebra, logic gates, combinational and sequential circuits, minimization techniques, and digital circuit design.

subjects.name: Data Structures & Algorithms
subjects.context: Arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, trees, graphs, sorting and searching algorithms with time and space complexity analysis.

subjects.name: Signals & Systems
subjects.context: Continuous and discrete signals, system properties, convolution, Fourier series and transforms with engineering applications.

subjects.name: Constitution of India
subjects.context: Fundamental rights, duties, governance structure, and ethical responsibilities.

Semester_4.focus: Understanding system-level design and interaction between hardware and software

subjects.name: Numerical Methods
subjects.context: Root finding, interpolation, numerical differentiation/integration, and solving differential equations using computational techniques.

subjects.name: Graph Theory
subjects.context: Graphs, trees, connectivity, shortest path algorithms, planar graphs, and applications in computing.

subjects.name: Operating Systems
subjects.context: Process management, CPU scheduling, deadlocks, memory management, file systems, and synchronization mechanisms.

subjects.name: Computer Organization & Architecture
subjects.context: Instruction sets, CPU organization, memory hierarchy, I/O systems, and performance evaluation.

subjects.name: Programming Languages
subjects.context: Syntax, semantics, parsing techniques, runtime environments, and comparison of programming paradigms.

subjects.name: Economics & Accountancy
subjects.context: Basic economic principles, cost analysis, and financial accounting relevant to engineering systems.

Semester_5.focus: Development of theoretical foundations and core system components

subjects.name: Probability & Random Processes
subjects.context: Probability theory, distributions, random variables, stochastic processes, and applications in computing systems.

subjects.name: Formal Language & Automata Theory
subjects.context: Regular expressions, DFA/NFA, context-free grammars, pushdown automata, and Turing machines.

subjects.name: Computer Graphics
subjects.context: 2D/3D transformations, viewing pipeline, rasterization, clipping, and basic rendering techniques.

subjects.name: Database Management Systems
subjects.context: Relational model, SQL, normalization, indexing, transactions, concurrency control, and recovery.

subjects.name: Microprocessor & Microcontroller
subjects.context: Architecture, instruction sets, assembly programming, interfacing, and embedded system basics.

subjects.name: Principles of Management
subjects.context: Management theories, planning, organizing, leadership, and decision-making processes.

Semester_6.focus: Advanced computing systems and software development methodologies

subjects.name: Computer Networks
subjects.context: OSI and TCP/IP models, routing algorithms, congestion control, data link protocols, and network security basics.

subjects.name: Software Engineering
subjects.context: Software development lifecycle, requirement analysis, design methodologies, testing, and maintenance.

subjects.name: Compiler Design
subjects.context: Lexical analysis, syntax parsing, semantic analysis, intermediate code generation, optimization, and code generation.

subjects.name: Advanced Algorithms / Theory
subjects.context: Algorithm design paradigms such as divide and conquer, greedy methods, dynamic programming, and complexity analysis.

Semester_7.focus: Application of knowledge in secure and large-scale systems with industry orientation

subjects.name: Cryptography & Information Security
subjects.context: Symmetric/asymmetric encryption, hashing, digital signatures, authentication, and network security protocols.

subjects.name: Advanced Software Engineering
subjects.context: Software architecture, design patterns, project management, and quality assurance.

subjects.name: Electives
subjects.context: Specialized domains such as AI, data science, distributed systems, or other emerging technologies.

subjects.name: Humanities Elective
subjects.context: Ethics, intellectual property rights, and professional responsibility.

subjects.name: Project / Internship
subjects.context: Real-world problem solving, system design, implementation, and documentation.

subjects.name: Program Electives
subjects.context: Advanced topics such as cryptography, natural language processing, and parallel computing.

subjects.name: Advanced Electives
subjects.context: Emerging areas including big data analytics, computer vision, and deep learning.

subjects.name: Open Electives
subjects.context: Interdisciplinary subjects such as AI, IoT, quantum computing, and soft computing.

subjects.name: Major Project
subjects.context: Capstone project involving design, development, and evaluation of a complete system.
